title: Claude code builds custom gpt
note: using Claude Code to design and package a ChatGPT custom GPT — cross-tool workflow, revisit after trying it at work

turns.text:
Done. Zip ready at:

`~/Documents/Claude code\scratch\process-flow-gpt.zip`

Contents: top-level README explaining the A/B choice, plus two self-contained folders. Each folder has `instructions.md` (paste into Instructions field), `mermaid-style-guide.md` + `elicitation-patterns.md` (upload as Knowledge), and a README with 5-minute setup steps. Conversation starters set to your three. Export guidance is in the instructions — the GPT shows it to users in plain language (save image, paste code into mermaid.live, drop into slides/docs) the first time it renders in a conversation.

Recommendation in the top-level README defaults to Option A (mermaid.ink) for cross-client reliability; B is there if you want zero external dependencies.

<command-message>bookmark</command-message>
<command-name>/bookmark</command-name>
<command-args>Fun example of using claude code to make a chatGPT custom GPT. Also add as a potential writing topic for the future, once I've tried it and reflected on the results</command-args>
